mission.title: jaeger: 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT
mission.description: 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

Query outputs:
"error":"cannot obtain reporter config from env: cannot parse env var JAEGER_AGENT_PORT=udp://10.90.32.41:5775

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from

steps.title: Apply the fix for 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT
steps.description:
> Note that this happens only when the Jaeger instance is called `jaeger`, which is arguably a very common name for the Jaeger instance:
> 
> * [grafana/loki#480](https://github.com/grafana/loki/issues/480)
> * [observatorium/configuration#127](https://github.com/observatorium/configuration/pull/127)
> 
> One possible solution is to ignore the env var value if the value is not an integer, without breaking the agent because of it.
> 
> Question: do we want to address this in the Jaeger Go

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT" is functioning correctly.
